level_signals: Comp range $290K-$365K base, consistent with senior IC at Anthropic
'Several years of experience in technical program management'
Owns inference deployment roadmap and engages with engineering leadership

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Have several years of experience in technical program management, with proven success delivering complex infrastructure programs, preferably in ML/AI systems or large-scale distributed systems
Have deep technical understanding of inference systems, compilers, or hardware accelerators to engage substantively with engineers and identify technical risks.
Excel at creating structure and processes in ambiguous environments, bringing clarity to complex cross-team initiatives
Have strong stakeholder management skills and can build trust with both technical and non-technical partners
Are comfortable navigating competing priorities and using data to drive technical decisions
Have experience with infrastructure scaling initiatives, hardware integrations, or deployment governance
Thrive in fast-paced environments and can balance strategic planning with tactical execution
Are passionate about AI infrastructure and understand the unique challenges of deploying and scaling large language models
Bachelor's degree or an equivalent combination of education, training, and/or experience

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: with proven success delivering complex infrastructure programs, preferably in ML/AI systems or large-scale distributed systems

scope.surface: Inference runtime and accelerator performance infrastructure; multi-hardware platform launches and cross-functional coordination
scope.business_model: internal infrastructure / platform team supporting AI research and product
core_responsibilities: Lead cross-functional initiatives for new infrastructure integration, establishing clear ownership, timelines, and communication channels between teams
Drive end-to-end planning for major infrastructure transitions including platform modernization and new tech adoption
Partner with engineering teams to identify optimization opportunities, track performance metrics, and prioritize work that unlocks capacity gains
Drive end-to-end readiness for model and feature launches across multiple hardware platforms
Own and prioritize the inference deployment roadmap, working closely with engineering leadership
Build strong relationships across research, engineering, and product teams
Translate technical complexities into clear updates for leadership
Identify inefficiencies in current workflows and drive systematic improvements
Establish metrics and dashboards to track infrastructure health, capacity utilization, and deployment success rates

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and highly competitive ($290K-$365K base)
Visa sponsorship explicitly offered with immigration lawyer support
Clear scope: inference runtime and accelerator performance with well-defined responsibilities
Rolling applications with no deadline pressure
Encourages applications from candidates who don't meet every qualification
Explicit DEI commitment and underrepresented group outreach
red_flags: Hybrid policy requires at least 25% office time; San Francisco only — eliminates fully-remote candidates
Minimum years of experience field defers to 'internal job level requirements' without specifying a number — ambiguous bar
Very wide comp range ($290K-$365K, $75K spread) suggests significant level variance; actual level expectations are unclear
